description: Core prescribing cascade knowledge base for clinical decision support — DEV build
disclaimer: For decision support only. Always verify with current clinical guidelines.

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Inhibición de prostaglandinas → retención de sodio/agua → vasoconstriccción → elevación de PA
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Prostaglandin inhibition → sodium/water retention + vasoconstriction → BP elevation
cascades.ade_es: Elevación de tensión arterial
cascades.ade_en: Blood pressure elevation
differential_hints: Essential hypertension poorly controlled prior to NSAID initiation
White coat hypertension (24h ambulatory monitoring may clarify)
High dietary sodium intake as primary driver
Obesity-related hypertension independent of NSAID
Renal parenchymal disease causing secondary hypertension
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar si los AINEs son realmente necesarios. En PVVIH, preferir paracetamol cuando sea posible.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider if NSAIDs are truly necessary. In PLHIV, prefer paracetamol when possible.
references: Scott IA et al. Med J Aust 2013
Lanas A et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther 2003

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Inhibición de COX-1 gástrica → reducción de moco protector → irritación/úlcera GI
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Gastric COX-1 inhibition → reduced protective mucus → GI irritation/ulceration
cascades.ade_es: Dispepsia, úlcera péptica, náuseas, dolor abdominal
cascades.ade_en: Dyspepsia, peptic ulcer, nausea, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Pre-existing GERD or peptic ulcer disease independent of NSAID
H. pylori infection as primary peptic ulcer cause
Functional dyspepsia unrelated to NSAID use
Stress-related mucosal disease in critically ill patients
Alcohol or dietary triggers for dyspepsia
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Si se suspende el AINE, el IBP puede no ser necesario. En PVVIH verificar interacciones de IBP con ARV.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If NSAID is stopped, the PPI may not be needed. In PLHIV verify PPI-ARV interactions.

cascades.name_es: IECA → Tos → Antitusivo / Cambio a ARA-II
cascades.name_en: ACE Inhibitor → Cough → Antitussive / Switch to ARB
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Acumulación de bradicinina → irritación de vías respiratorias → tos seca
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bradykinin accumulation → respiratory tract irritation → dry cough
cascades.ade_es: Tos seca persistente
cascades.ade_en: Persistent dry cough
differential_hints: Viral upper respiratory tract infection as cause of acute cough
Post-nasal drip syndrome or allergic rhinitis
GERD-related chronic cough
Asthma or eosinophilic bronchitis
Cardiac failure with pulmonary congestion causing cough
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La solución correcta es cambiar el IECA por un ARA-II, no añadir antitusivo.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: The correct solution is to switch ACE inhibitor to ARB, not add an antitussive.

cascades.name_es: Antagonista del calcio → Edema maleolar → Diurético
cascades.name_en: Calcium Channel Blocker → Ankle Edema → Diuretic
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Vasodilatación arteriolar preferente → incremento de presión capilar → edema periférico
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Preferential arteriolar vasodilation → increased capillary pressure → peripheral edema
cascades.ade_es: Edema de tobillos/piernas
cascades.ade_en: Ankle/leg edema
differential_hints: Cardiac failure causing peripheral edema
Chronic venous insufficiency independent of CCB
Hypoalbuminemia (nephrotic syndrome, liver disease)
Lymphedema
Deep vein thrombosis—must be excluded clinically
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar reducción de dosis del ACa, cambio a dihidropiridina de menor efecto vasodilatador, o adición de IECA/ARA-II.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider reducing CCB dose, switching to less vasodilatory dihydropyridine, or adding ACEi/ARB.

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Resistencia a insulina inducida por glucocorticoides → hiperglucemia
cascades.ade_es: Hiperglucemia, diabetes esteroidea
cascades.ade_en: Hyperglycemia, steroid-induced diabetes
differential_hints: Pre-existing type 2 diabetes worsening due to stress or weight gain
Pre-diabetes unmasked by steroid-induced insulin resistance
Concurrent high-calorie intake post-procedure or during illness
Pancreatic insufficiency or pancreatitis as independent cause
Acromegaly or Cushing's syndrome as independent endocrine disorder
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Revisar si el corticoide puede reducirse o suspenderse. La insulina suele ser más apropiada que la metformina para la hiperglucemia esteroidea.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Review whether corticosteroid can be reduced or discontinued. Insulin is often more appropriate than metformin for steroid-induced hyperglycemia.
references: Clore JN, Thurby-Hay L. Ann Pharmacother 2009

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Inhibición de osteoblastos + activación de osteoclastos → pérdida ósea
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Osteoblast inhibition + osteoclast activation → bone loss
cascades.ade_es: Osteoporosis inducida por corticoides
cascades.ade_en: Corticosteroid-induced osteoporosis
differential_hints: Age-related or post-menopausal bone loss as primary cause
Vitamin D and calcium deficiency independent of corticosteroid
Secondary hyperparathyroidism due to CKD
Multiple myeloma or bone metastasis mimicking osteoporosis
Hyperthyroidism or excessive levothyroxine causing bone loss
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Puede ser una prescripción apropiada si el corticoide es necesario a largo plazo. Evaluar el contexto clínico y la densitometría ósea.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: May be an appropriate prescription if corticosteroid is needed long-term. Evaluate clinical context and bone density before starting or withholding bisphosphonate.
references: Weinstein RS. Endocrinol Metab Clin North Am 2012

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Reducción de excreción renal de urato → hiperuricemia → ataque de gota
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Reduced renal urate excretion → hyperuricemia → gout attack
cascades.ade_es: Hiperuricemia, artritis gotosa
cascades.ade_en: Hyperuricemia, gouty arthritis
differential_hints: Primary gout (idiopathic hyperuricemia) independent of thiazide use
Dietary purine excess (red meat, seafood, alcohol)
Chronic kidney disease with reduced urate excretion
Septic arthritis—must be excluded by joint aspiration
Pseudogout (calcium pyrophosphate deposition) mimicking gout
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Si la tiazida puede suspenderse, los niveles de urato podrían normalizarse. Evaluar la indicación del diurético.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If thiazide can be stopped, urate levels may normalize. Evaluate the diuretic indication.

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Activación de receptores mu opioides en plexo entérico → reducción del peristaltismo → estreñimiento
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Activation of mu-opioid receptors in enteric plexus → reduced peristalsis → constipation
cascades.ade_es: Estreñimiento inducido por opioides
cascades.ade_en: Opioid-induced constipation (OIC)
differential_hints: Underlying colorectal pathology—must not be missed in new constipation
Dehydration or low dietary fiber compounding opioid effect
Concurrent anticholinergic medications as additive cause
Hypothyroidism causing independent constipation
Immobility from pain or post-operative status
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: El laxante es apropiado y debe iniciarse profilácticamente al comenzar opioides. Preferir laxantes osmóticos o estimulantes; evaluar si el opioide puede reducirse. En estreñimiento refractario considerar naloxegol o metilnaltrexona.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Laxative is appropriate and should be started prophylactically when opioids are initiated. Prefer osmotic or stimulant laxatives; evaluate if opioid can be reduced. For refractory OIC consider naloxegol or methylnaltrexone.

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Bloqueo dopaminérgico D2 en vía nigroestriada → síntomas extrapiramidales
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: D2 dopaminergic blockade in nigrostriatal pathway → extrapyramidal symptoms
cascades.ade_es: Parkinsonismo, distonía, acatisia
cascades.ade_en: Parkinsonism, dystonia, akathisia
differential_hints: Idiopathic Parkinson's disease (distinguish from drug-induced parkinsonism)
Other drugs with dopamine-blocking properties (metoclopramide, prochlorperazine)
Akathisia misidentified as anxiety or agitation requiring dose increase
Tardive dyskinesia (may be worsened by anticholinergics)
Psychomotor retardation from underlying psychiatric illness
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Los anticolinérgicos tienen muchos efectos adversos propios. La solución preferida es cambiar a un antipsiccótico con menor riesgo de síntomas extrapiramidales. Evitar en mayores.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Anticholinergics have many adverse effects. Preferred solution is switching to an antipsychotic with lower EPS risk. Avoid in elderly patients.
references: Kane JM et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2009

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Activación serotoninérgica → estimulación central → insomnio, especialmente al inicio
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonergic activation → central stimulation → insomnia, especially at initiation
cascades.ade_es: Insomnio, alteraciones del sueño
cascades.ade_en: Insomnia, sleep disturbances
differential_hints: Insomnia as symptom of underlying unresolved major depressive disorder
Anxiety disorder independently causing sleep disturbance
Obstructive sleep apnea (central or mixed)
Restless legs syndrome or periodic limb movement disorder
Caffeine, alcohol, or screen use as behavioural contributors
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Los hipnóticos tienen riesgo de dependencia. Considerar higiene del sueño, cambiar la hora de administración del ISRS o cambiar de antidepresivo.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Hypnotics carry dependency risk. Consider sleep hygiene, changing SSRI timing, or switching antidepressant.
references: Fava M et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2006

cascades.name_es: Inhibidor de colinesterasa → Incontinencia urinaria → Anticolinérgico
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Exceso colinérgico vesical → contracciones involuntarias del detrusor → incontinencia
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bladder cholinergic excess → involuntary detrusor contractions → urge incontinence
cascades.ade_es: Incontinencia urinaria de urgencia, urgencia miccional
cascades.ade_en: Urge urinary incontinence, urinary urgency
differential_hints: Overactive bladder disease independent of cholinesterase inhibitor
Urinary tract infection causing urgency symptoms
Benign prostatic hyperplasia with urge component in men
Pelvic floor dysfunction in women
Interstitial cystitis or structural bladder pathology
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Cascada particularmente problemática: el anticolinérgico antagoniza directamente el inhibidor de colinesterasa. Evaluar alternativas (mirabegrón) antes de añadir un anticolinérgico.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Particularly problematic cascade: the anticholinergic directly antagonizes the cholinesterase inhibitor. Evaluate alternatives (mirabegron) before adding an anticholinergic.
references: Gill SS et al. Arch Intern Med 2005

cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Alteración de la microbiota intestinal y motilidad GI → diarrea, náuseas, dolor abdominal
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: GI microbiota alteration and motility changes → diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain
cascades.ade_es: Diarrea, náuseas, meteorismo, dolor abdominal
cascades.ade_en: Diarrhea, nausea, bloating, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Gastroenteritis or infectious diarrhea
IBD exacerbation (Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis)
Celiac disease or food intolerance
Other medications causing GI upset (antibiotics, NSAIDs)
Diabetic autonomic neuropathy with gastroparesis or diarrhea
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La solución preferida es la formulación de liberación prolongada o la reducción de dosis, no añadir un antidiarreico.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Preferred solution: extended-release formulation or dose reduction, not adding an antidiarrheal.

cascades.ade_es: Depresión, estado de ánimo bajo, fatiga, embotamiento emocional
cascades.ade_en: Depression, low mood, fatigue, emotional blunting
differential_hints: Underlying major depressive disorder independent of beta-blocker
Hypothyroidism causing depressive symptoms (check TSH)
Social isolation or situational psychosocial factors
Chronic heart failure causing fatigue misattributed to depression
Anemia or other medical comorbidities
Sleep apnea contributing to fatigue and low mood
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La evidencia de depresión inducida por betabloqueantes es debatida. Los agentes lipófilos (propranolol, metoprolol) tienen mayor penetración en el SNC. Considerar cambiar a un agente hidrófilo (atenolol) antes de añadir un antidepresivo.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Evidence for beta-blocker-induced depression is debated; lipophilic agents (propranolol, metoprolol) have higher CNS penetration. Consider switching to a hydrophilic agent (atenolol) before adding an antidepressant.

cascades.ade_es: Mialgia, debilidad muscular, miopatía, rabdomiolólisis (rara)
cascades.ade_en: Myalgia, muscle weakness, myopathy, rhabdomyolysis (rare)
differential_hints: Polymyalgia rheumatica (check ESR/CRP)
Viral myalgia
Physical deconditioning or overexertion
Hypothyroidism as independent cause (check TSH and CK)
Vitamin D deficiency
Inflammatory myopathy or dermatomyositis
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Verificar niveles de CK. Si se confirma mialgia inducida por estatina, considerar reducción de dosis, pausa del fármaco o cambio a una estatina menos miotóxica (pravastatina, fluvastatina). En PVVIH, los inhibidores de proteasa aumentan la exposición a estatinas.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Check CK levels. If statin-induced myalgia confirmed, consider dose reduction, drug holiday, or switch to less myotoxic statin (pravastatin, fluvastatin). In PLHIV, protease inhibitors increase statin exposure—drug interaction is critical.

cascades.name_es: Diurético de asa → Hipopotasemia → Suplemento de potasio
cascades.ade_es: Hipopotasemia, calambres musculares, debilidad, arritmia cardíaca
cascades.ade_en: Hypokalemia, muscle cramps, weakness, cardiac arrhythmia
differential_hints: Inadequate dietary potassium intake
Concurrent vomiting or diarrhea as additional potassium loss
Hyperaldosteronism (primary or secondary)
Hypomagnesemia preventing effective potassium correction
Digoxin toxicity potentiated by hypokalemia
Thiazide co-administration compounding potassium loss
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Corregir simultáneamente la hipomagnesemia: impide la repleción efectiva de potasio. Considerar un diurético ahorrador de potasio en lugar del suplemento oral si se requiere el diurético de asa de forma continua.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Hypomagnesemia must be corrected simultaneously—it prevents effective potassium repletion. Consider addition of a potassium-sparing diuretic instead of oral supplement if ongoing loop diuretic is needed.

cascades.name_es: IECA / ARA-II → Hiperpotasemia → Resina de intercambio catiónico
cascades.name_en: ACE Inhibitor / ARB → Hyperkalemia → Potassium Binder
cascades.ade_es: Hiperpotasemia, bradicardia, alteraciones de la conducción cardíaca
cascades.ade_en: Hyperkalemia, bradycardia, cardiac conduction abnormalities
differential_hints: CKD-related hyperkalemia independent of ACEi/ARB
Concurrent NSAID use reducing renal potassium excretion
Dietary potassium excess or potassium supplement over-supplementation
Potassium-sparing diuretic combination (aldosterone antagonist)
Type 4 renal tubular acidosis
Trimethoprim-induced hyperkalemia (relevant in PLHIV on TMP-SMX for PCP prophylaxis)
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Antes de añadir un quelante de potasio, reevaluar la dosis de IECA/ARA-II y considerar modificación dietética. En PVVIH, el trimetoprim-sulfametoxazol (profilaxis de PCP) también causa hiperpotasemia.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Before adding a potassium binder, reassess ACEi/ARB dose and consider dietary modification. In PLHIV, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (used for PCP prophylaxis) also causes hyperkalemia—additive risk warrants close monitoring.

cascades.ade_es: Hipotensión ortostática, mareo, presíncope, síncope, caídas
cascades.ade_en: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, presyncope, syncope, falls
differential_hints: Volume depletion or dehydration from inadequate fluid intake
Autonomic neuropathy (diabetes, Parkinson's disease)
Adrenal insufficiency
Polypharmacy with multiple hypotensive agents (additive effect)
Amyloidosis or other systemic autonomic dysfunction
Post-prandial hypotension in older adults
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La reducción o eliminación del fármaco es preferida a añadir vasopresores. La fludrocortisona conlleva riesgo de hipopotasemia y retención de líquidos; evitar en insuficiencia cardíaca. Medidas no farmacológicas primero.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Dose reduction or medication elimination is preferred over adding vasopressors. Fludrocortisone carries hypokalemia and fluid retention risk; avoid in heart failure. Non-pharmacological measures (head-of-bed elevation, compression stockings, hydration) first.

cascades.ade_es: Hipotiroidismo, fatiga, aumento de peso, intolerancia al frío, empeoramiento de bradicardia
cascades.ade_en: Hypothyroidism, fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, bradycardia worsening
differential_hints: Autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto's) as independent cause
Prior thyroid pathology or thyroid surgery
Iodine excess from radiographic contrast agents or diet
Subclinical hypothyroidism evolving independently
Drug-induced thyroiditis from other agents (lithium, interferon)
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La amiodarona contiene ~37% de yodo en peso y tiene una vida media de 40-55 días; se requiere monitorización tiroidea durante años tras suspensión. El hipotiroidismo puede tratarse mientras la amiodarona continúa si el control de la arritmia es crítico.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Amiodarone contains ~37% iodine by weight and has a half-life of 40-55 days; thyroid monitoring is needed for years after discontinuation. Hypothyroidism may be treated while amiodarone continues if arrhythmia control is critical.

cascades.name_en: Benzodiazepine / Z-Drug → Falls / Fractures → Bone Treatment
cascades.ade_es: Sedación, deterioro cognitivo, inestabilidad de la marcha, caídas, fracturas de cadera
cascades.ade_en: Sedation, cognitive impairment, gait instability, falls, hip fractures
differential_hints: Environmental hazards (poor lighting, loose rugs, unsafe bathroom)
Cognitive impairment or dementia as primary fall cause
Vestibular disorder or benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV)
Parkinson's disease or other movement disorder
Orthostatic hypotension from other medications
Muscle weakness from deconditioning, sarcopenia, or vitamin D deficiency
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Las benzodiacepinas y fármacos-Z figuran en los Criterios de Beers en adultos mayores. La intervención correcta es la retirada gradual, no tratar el riesgo de fractura aguas abajo mientras se perpetúa la causa.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Benzodiazepines and Z-drugs are Beers Criteria drugs in older adults. Gradual tapering and discontinuation is the correct intervention, not treating the downstream fracture risk while perpetuating the cause.

cascades.ade_es: Retención urinaria, incontinencia por rebosamiento, obstrucción del tracto de salida vesical
cascades.ade_en: Urinary retention, overflow incontinence, bladder outlet obstruction
differential_hints: Benign prostatic hyperplasia as independent cause (particularly in older men)
High cumulative anticholinergic burden from multiple medications
Urethral stricture
Neurogenic bladder from diabetes or spinal cord pathology
Postoperative urinary retention
Pelvic organ prolapse in women
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar cambio del ATC a un ISRS o IRSN con mucha menor carga anticolinérgica. En hombres mayores con HBP, los ATC son de alto riesgo. Añadir un alfabloqueante perpetúa la causa anticolinérgica sin abordarla.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching TCA to an SSRI or SNRI with much lower anticholinergic burden. In older men with BPH, TCAs are particularly high-risk. Adding an alpha-blocker perpetuates the anticholinergic cause without addressing it.

cascades.name_es: Gabapentinoide → Mareo/Caídas → Analgésico por lesión de caída
cascades.name_en: Gabapentinoid → Dizziness / Falls → Analgesic for Fall Injury
cascades.ade_es: Mareo, somnolencia, ataxia, edema periférico, caídas
cascades.ade_en: Dizziness, somnolence, ataxia, peripheral edema, falls
differential_hints: Meniere's disease or labyrinthitis
Vertebrobasilar insufficiency
Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV)
Orthostatic hypotension from other medications
Underlying neurological disorder (cerebellar, peripheral neuropathy)
Polypharmacy interactions with other CNS depressants (opioids, benzodiazepines)
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Los gabapentinoides son de alto riesgo en pacientes mayores, especialmente a dosis altas o con insuficiencia renal. Reducir la dosis o suspender antes de tratar las lesiones por caída con analgésicos adicionales.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Gabapentinoids are increasingly recognized as high-risk in elderly patients, especially at higher doses or with renal impairment. Dose reduction or discontinuation should be considered before treating fall-related injuries with additional analgesics.

cascades.name_es: Antipsiccótico atípico → Síndrome metabólico/Ganancia de peso → Metformina
cascades.name_en: Atypical Antipsychotic → Metabolic Syndrome / Weight Gain → Metformin
cascades.ade_es: Ganancia de peso, síndrome metabólico, dislipidemia, alteración de la tolerancia a la glucosa
cascades.ade_en: Weight gain, metabolic syndrome, dyslipidemia, impaired glucose tolerance
differential_hints: Dietary habits and sedentary lifestyle as primary drivers
Depression-related hyperphagia and weight changes
Hypothyroidism contributing to weight gain
Pre-existing metabolic syndrome before antipsychotic initiation
Corticosteroid co-administration compounding metabolic effects
Psychiatric illness itself affecting eating regulation
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar cambio a un antipsiccótico metabólicamente neutro (aripiprazol, ziprasidona, amisulprida) antes de añadir agentes metabólicos. En PVVIH, considerar también la contribución de los ARV a la lipodistrofia.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to a metabolically neutral antipsychotic (aripiprazole, ziprasidone, amisulpride) before adding metabolic agents. In PLHIV, also consider ARV contribution to lipodystrophy and metabolic syndrome.

cascades.name_es: Levodopa / Agonista dopaminérgico → Hipotensión ortostática → Vasopresor
cascades.name_en: Levodopa / Dopamine Agonist → Orthostatic Hypotension → Vasopressor
cascades.ade_es: Hipotensión ortostática, síncope, caídas, mareo al ponerse de pie
cascades.ade_en: Orthostatic hypotension, syncope, falls, dizziness on standing
differential_hints: Parkinson's disease-related autonomic dysfunction (independent of medications)
Volume depletion or inadequate fluid intake
Co-administration of antihypertensives compounding hypotension
Cardiac dysfunction with reduced output
Dehydration secondary to dysphagia or swallowing difficulties
Multiple system atrophy (severe autonomic involvement)
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La EP en sí causa disfunción autonómica; diferenciar el efecto del fármaco de la progresión de la enfermedad es difícil. Las intervenciones no farmacológicas deben preceder al tratamiento farmacológico.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Parkinson's disease itself causes autonomic dysfunction; disentangling drug effect from disease progression is challenging. Non-pharmacological interventions (head-of-bed elevation, physical countermaneuvers, compression garments) should precede pharmacological treatment.

cascades.name_es: Antihistamínico de primera generación → Deterioro cognitivo → Inhibidor de colinesterasa
cascades.name_en: First-Generation Antihistamine → Cognitive Impairment → Cholinesterase Inhibitor
cascades.ade_es: Deterioro cognitivo anticolinérgico, confusión, delirium, quejas de memoria aguda
cascades.ade_en: Anticholinergic-induced cognitive impairment, confusion, delirium, acute memory complaints
differential_hints: Early Alzheimer's disease or other dementia onset
Delirium from infection (UTI, pneumonia, sepsis)
Depression presenting as pseudodementia
Other anticholinergic medications in polypharmacy (cumulative burden)
Metabolic encephalopathy (hyponatremia, hypothyroidism, hepatic failure)
Normal cognitive aging misinterpreted as pathological
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Cascada especialmente perjudicial: los antihistamínicos de primera generación tienen muy alta carga anticolinérgica y antagonizan directamente los inhibidores de colinesterasa. Suspender el antihistamínico puede resolver completamente el deterioro cognitivo aparente.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Particularly harmful cascade: first-generation antihistamines have very high anticholinergic burden and directly antagonize cholinesterase inhibitors. Stopping the antihistamine may fully resolve apparent cognitive decline—a therapeutic trial before prescribing dementia drugs.

cascades.ade_es: Estreñimiento, meteorismo, esfuerzo defecatorio, impactación fecal
cascades.ade_en: Constipation, bloating, straining, fecal impaction
differential_hints: Dietary fiber deficiency or inadequate fluid intake
Concurrent opioid use as primary or additive cause
Other anticholinergic medications in polypharmacy (cumulative GI effect)
Hypothyroidism
Colorectal pathology (do not miss structural cause)
Immobility or deconditioning
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar mirabegrón (agonista beta-3) como alternativa a los agentes urinarios anticolinérgicos—eficacia similar con menor estreñimiento y carga cognitiva. Evaluar la carga anticolinérgica acumulada en pacientes mayores.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider mirabegron (beta-3 agonist) as an alternative to anticholinergic urinary agents—similar efficacy with lower constipation and cognitive burden. Cumulative anticholinergic burden across all medications must be assessed in older patients.

cascades.name_es: Alfabloqueante → Hipotensión ortostática → Vasopresor / Tratamiento de lesión por caída
cascades.name_en: Alpha-1 Blocker → Orthostatic Hypotension → Vasopressor / Fall Injury Treatment
cascades.ade_es: Hipotensión ortostática, mareo, síncope, caídas, lesiones por caída
cascades.ade_en: Orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, syncope, falls, fall-related injury
differential_hints: Volume depletion from concurrent diuretic use
Antihypertensive polypharmacy with additive hypotensive effect
Autonomic neuropathy from diabetes
BPH as the primary indication driving continued use
PDE5 inhibitor co-administration (dangerous additive hypotension)
Dehydration from warm climate or inadequate intake
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Los alfabloqueantes para la HBP en hombres mayores causan frecuentemente hipotensión ortostática a la primera dosis. Considerar inhibidores de 5-alfa reductasa o agonistas beta-3. Si se continúan, iniciar a dosis muy baja al acostarse.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Alpha-1 blockers for BPH in older men frequently cause first-dose and positional orthostatic hypotension. Consider 5-alpha reductase inhibitor or beta-3 agonist alternatives. If continued, start at very low dose at bedtime and counsel on rising slowly.

cascades.ade_es: Delirium, confusión aguda, agitación, alucinaciones, paranoia
cascades.ade_en: Delirium, acute confusion, agitation, hallucinations, paranoia
differential_hints: Infection-related delirium (UTI, pneumonia, sepsis)—most common cause in elderly
Metabolic derangement (hyponatremia, hypoglycemia, hepatic failure)
Alcohol or benzodiazepine withdrawal state
Cerebrovascular event (stroke or TIA)
Underlying dementia with behavioural and psychological symptoms
Hypoxia or respiratory failure
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Identificar y retirar el fármaco anticolinérgico ofensivo primero—esto por sí solo puede resolver el delirium. Los antipsiccóticos para el manejo del delirium pueden ser apropiados a corto plazo, pero tienen su propia carga anticolinérgica.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Identify and remove the offending anticholinergic drug first—this alone may resolve delirium. Antipsychotics for delirium management may be appropriate short-term but have their own anticholinergic burden, potentially worsening the root cause.

cascades.name_es: Diurético de asa → Urgencia urinaria/Nicturia → Anticolinérgico
cascades.name_en: Loop Diuretic → Urinary Urgency / Nocturia → Anticholinergic
cascades.ade_es: Urgencia urinaria, frecuencia, nicturia (diuresis farmacológica atribuida incorrectamente a vejiga hiperactiva)
cascades.ade_en: Urinary urgency, frequency, nocturia (pharmacological diuresis misattributed to overactive bladder)
differential_hints: True overactive bladder disease independent of diuretic
Interstitial cystitis
Urinary tract infection
Benign prostatic hyperplasia with urge component
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus causing osmotic diuresis
High caffeine or fluid intake
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Cambiar el momento de administración del diurético (p.ej., solo por la mañana, evitar la tarde) puede resolver la nicturia sin añadir ningún medicamento nuevo. Se recomienda evaluación urológica antes de iniciar un anticolinérgico.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Repositioning the time of diuretic administration (e.g., morning dose only, avoiding evening) may resolve nocturia without adding any new medication. Urological evaluation is warranted before starting an anticholinergic agent.

cascades.name_es: IBP crónico → Hipomagnesemia → Suplemento de magnesio
cascades.ade_es: Hipomagnesemia, calambres musculares, tetania, arritmia cardíaca, hipopotasemia refractaria
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesemia, muscle cramps, tetany, cardiac arrhythmia, refractory hypokalemia
differential_hints: Malabsorption syndrome (celiac disease, Crohn's)
Chronic diarrhea from other causes
Alcoholism or alcohol use disorder
Loop diuretic co-administration (CORE-038) with additive magnesium wasting
Diabetes mellitus with renal magnesium wasting
Hungry bone syndrome post-parathyroidectomy
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La FDA emitió una comunicación de seguridad en 2011 sobre la hipomagnesemia inducida por IBP. La suplementación oral puede ser insuficiente si el IBP continúa; puede ser necesario cambiar a bloqueante H2. La hipopotasemia coexistente no se corrige hasta que se normaliza el magnesio.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: FDA issued a safety communication in 2011 about PPI-induced hypomagnesemia. Oral supplementation may be insufficient if PPI is continued; switching from PPI to H2 blocker may be necessary. Co-existing hypokalemia is resistant to correction until magnesium is normalized.

cascades.name_es: IBP crónico → Deficiencia de vitamina B12 → Cianocobalamina
cascades.ade_es: Deficiencia de vitamina B12, anemia macroccítica, neuropatía periférica, deterioro cognitivo
cascades.ade_en: Vitamin B12 deficiency, macrocytic anemia, peripheral neuropathy, cognitive impairment
differential_hints: Dietary deficiency (veganism, restrictive diet)
Pernicious anemia with intrinsic factor deficiency
Crohn's disease or ileal resection impairing absorption
Metformin-induced B12 depletion (additive with PPI, very common in PLHIV)
Atrophic gastritis as primary gastric cause
Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La deficiencia de B12 por IBP es más común con uso prolongado (>2 años) y dosis altas. En adultos mayores, las manifestaciones neurológicas pueden preceder a la anemia. En PVVIH, la deficiencia de B12 puede empeorar la neuropatía periférica relacionada con ARV.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: B12 deficiency from PPIs is more common with long-term use (>2 years) and higher doses. In older adults, neurological manifestations may precede anemia. In PLHIV, B12 deficiency can worsen ARV-related peripheral neuropathy—important cascade to detect.

cascades.ade_es: Náuseas, vómitos, gastroparesia, vaciado gástrico enlentecido
cascades.ade_en: Nausea, vomiting, gastroparesis, delayed gastric emptying
differential_hints: Underlying GI pathology (gastric outlet obstruction, gastroparesis)
Vestibular origin nausea (labyrinthitis, motion sickness)
Chemotherapy-induced nausea in oncology patients (CINV)
Anxiety or psychogenic nausea
Hypercalcemia from malignancy or immobility
Constipation-related nausea (opioid-induced, see CC008)
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Las náuseas inducidas por opioides suelen aparecer al inicio y frecuentemente se resuelven en días—la espera vigilante es apropiada. El metoclopramida puede empeorar el riesgo de síntomas extrapiramidales en pacientes con antipsiccóticos.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Opioid-induced nausea typically occurs at initiation and often resolves within days—watchful waiting is appropriate. Metoclopramide may worsen EPS risk in patients already on antipsychotics. In PLHIV, antiretroviral-related nausea may compound opioid-induced nausea.

cascades.name_es: Hierro oral → Estreñimiento/Malestar GI → Laxante o Antiácido
cascades.name_en: Oral Iron Supplement → Constipation / GI Upset → Laxative or Antacid
cascades.ade_es: Estreñimiento, náuseas, calambres abdominales, heces oscuras, malestar GI
cascades.ade_en: Constipation, nausea, abdominal cramps, dark stools, GI upset
differential_hints: Underlying constipation from other causes (opioids, anticholinergics)
Hypothyroidism as concurrent contributor
Low-fiber diet or inadequate hydration
Celiac disease or GI malabsorption causing GI symptoms
Colorectal cancer (important not to miss in new GI symptoms)
IBD exacerbation
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar formulaciones alternativas de hierro (carboximaltosa férrica IV, dosificación oral en días alternos, formulaciones de menor dosis) para reducir los efectos secundarios GI. Añadir antiácidos o IBP paradójicamente reduce la absorción del hierro.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider alternative iron formulations (ferric carboxymaltose IV, alternate-day oral dosing, lower-dose formulations) to reduce GI side effects. Adding antacids or PPIs paradoxically reduces iron absorption, defeating the purpose of supplementation.

cascades.ade_es: Hipertensión, retención de sodio y agua, mayor riesgo cardiovascular
cascades.ade_en: Hypertension, sodium and water retention, elevated cardiovascular risk
differential_hints: Essential hypertension poorly controlled before corticosteroid initiation
Pain-related or anxiety-related blood pressure elevation
White coat hypertension
Obstructive sleep apnea as an independent cause
Renal artery stenosis
NSAID co-administration further worsening blood pressure
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Distinto de CC005 (cascada de hiperglucemia). La hipertensión inducida por corticoides es dependiente de la dosis; la fludrocortisona tiene el mayor efecto mineralocorticoide. La minimización de la dosis es preferida antes de añadir antihipertensivo.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Distinct from CC005 (hyperglycemia cascade). Corticosteroid-induced hypertension is dose-dependent; fludrocortisone has the greatest mineralocorticoid effect. Corticosteroid dose minimization is preferred before adding antihypertensive. ACEi/ARB preferred if microalbuminuria is present.

cascades.name_es: Corticosteroide → Edema periférico/Retención de líquidos → Diurético
cascades.name_en: Corticosteroid → Peripheral Edema / Fluid Retention → Diuretic
cascades.ade_es: Edema periférico, retención de líquidos, aumento de peso
cascades.ade_en: Peripheral edema, fluid retention, weight gain
differential_hints: Cardiac failure causing peripheral edema
Calcium channel blocker-related edema (see CC004)
Chronic venous insufficiency
Hypoalbuminemia causing distributive edema
Lymphedema
Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)—important to exclude
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: El edema por corticoides es frecuentemente dependiente de la dosis. La restricción de sodio y la minimización de la dosis del corticoide son preferidas a añadir diuréticos. Añadir un diurético aumenta el riesgo de hipopotasemia.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Edema from corticosteroids is often dose-dependent. Sodium restriction and corticosteroid dose minimization are preferred over adding diuretics. Adding a diuretic increases hypokalemia risk, especially if patient is also on a loop diuretic.

cascades.ade_es: Hiperlipidemia, hipertrigliceridemia, mayor riesgo cardiovascular
cascades.ade_en: Hyperlipidemia, hypertriglyceridemia, increased cardiovascular risk
differential_hints: Primary hyperlipidemia (familial hypercholesterolemia)
Dietary habits and sedentary lifestyle as dominant factors
Hypothyroidism causing secondary dyslipidemia (check TSH)
Diabetes mellitus-related dyslipidemia
Nephrotic syndrome
Protease inhibitor-related dyslipidemia in PLHIV
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Considerar cambio a un antipsiccótico metabólicamente neutro (aripiprazol, amisulprida) antes de añadir una estatina. En PVVIH, las interacciones estatina-ARV son críticas: la simvastatina está contraindicada con IP.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to a metabolically neutral antipsychotic (aripiprazole, amisulpride) before adding a statin. In PLHIV, statin-ARV interactions are critical: simvastatin is contraindicated with PIs; pravastatin or rosuvastatin are preferred.

cascades.name_es: Tiazida → Hiponatremia → Suplemento de sodio / Gestión de líquidos
cascades.name_en: Thiazide → Hyponatremia → Sodium Supplement / Fluid Management
cascades.ade_es: Hiponatremia, letargo, deterioro cognitivo, caídas, convulsiones (casos graves)
cascades.ade_en: Hyponatremia, lethargy, cognitive impairment, falls, seizures (severe cases)
differential_hints: SIADH from other medications or CNS/pulmonary disease
Heart failure-related dilutional hyponatremia
SSRI/SNRI co-administration (major additive SIADH risk, see CORE-039)
Hypothyroidism
Adrenal insufficiency
Polydipsia or excessive water intake
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La hiponatremia inducida por tiazidas es más común en mujeres mayores y ocurre frecuentemente en las primeras semanas. La suspensión de la tiazida es la intervención primaria. Evitar corrección rápida (>8 mEq/L por 24h) para prevenir el síndrome de desmielinización osmótica.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Thiazide-induced hyponatremia is most common in elderly women and often occurs within first weeks of starting or dose increase. Discontinuing the thiazide is the primary intervention. Avoid rapid correction (>8 mEq/L per 24h) to prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome.

cascades.name_es: Diurético de asa → Hipomagnesemia → Suplemento de magnesio
cascades.ade_es: Hipomagnesemia, calambres musculares, hipopotasemia refractaria, arritmia cardíaca
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesemia, muscle cramps, refractory hypokalemia, cardiac arrhythmia
differential_hints: Malabsorption syndromes
Alcoholism or chronic alcohol use disorder
PPI co-administration (CORE-029) with additive magnesium wasting effect
Diabetes mellitus with renal tubular magnesium wasting
Aminoglycoside nephrotoxicity
Chronic diarrhea or short bowel syndrome
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La hipomagnesemia causa hipopotasemia refractaria—ambos electrolitos deben corregirse juntos. Los diuréticos ahorradores de potasio (amilorida) también ahorran magnesio y pueden ser preferidos cuando es clínicamente apropiado.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Hypomagnesemia causes refractory hypokalemia—both electrolytes must be corrected together. Loop diuretics are the most common drug cause of hypomagnesemia. Potassium-sparing diuretics (amiloride) also spare magnesium and may be preferred where clinically appropriate.

cascades.name_es: ISRS / IRSN → Hiponatremia (SIADH) → Suplemento de sodio
cascades.ade_es: Hiponatremia mediada por SIADH, confusión, deterioro cognitivo, caídas, convulsiones
cascades.ade_en: SIADH-mediated hyponatremia, confusion, cognitive impairment, falls, seizures
differential_hints: Thiazide diuretic co-administration (major additive risk, CORE-037)
CNS disease causing SIADH (stroke, tumor, meningitis)
Pulmonary disease causing SIADH (pneumonia, TB, SCLC)
Hypothyroidism
Adrenal insufficiency
Heart failure-related dilutional hyponatremia
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: La hiponatremia inducida por ISRS es más común en pacientes mayores, especialmente en las primeras semanas. La paroxetina tiene riesgo anticolinérgico adicional. La tiazida concomitante amplifica en gran medida el riesgo. La reducción o el cambio de antidepresivo es la intervención primaria.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: SSRI-induced hyponatremia is most common in elderly patients, especially within the first weeks of starting or dose increase. Paroxetine carries additional anticholinergic risk. Concurrent thiazide greatly amplifies hyponatremia risk. Antidepressant dose reduction or switch is the primary intervention.

cascades.name_es: Antiepiléptico inductor enzimático → Deficiencia de vitamina D/Osteomalacia → Suplemento de vitamina D
cascades.name_en: Enzyme-Inducing Antiepileptic → Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia → Vitamin D Supplement
cascades.ade_es: Deficiencia de vitamina D, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, mayor riesgo de fractura
cascades.ade_en: Vitamin D deficiency, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, increased fracture risk
differential_hints: Dietary vitamin D deficiency from inadequate intake or sun exposure
Malabsorption syndrome (celiac disease, inflammatory bowel disease)
Chronic kidney disease with impaired 1-alpha-hydroxylation
Primary hypoparathyroidism
Age-related decline in cutaneous vitamin D synthesis
Post-bariatric surgery fat-soluble vitamin malabsorption
cascades.recommended_first_action_es: Los antiepilépticos inductores enzimáticos aceleran el catabolismo de la vitamina D por inducción del CYP450. Se recomienda monitorización de la densidad mineral ósea en usuarios a largo plazo. En PVVIH, estos antiepilépticos también pueden acelerar el metabolismo de los ARV.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Enzyme-inducing antiepileptics accelerate vitamin D catabolism via CYP450 induction. Bone mineral density monitoring is recommended in long-term users. In PLHIV, these antiepileptics may also accelerate ARV metabolism (especially NNRTIs, PIs), creating complex pharmacokinetic drug interactions.

non_cascade_iatrogenic.name_es: Antibiótico de amplio espectro → Infección por C. difficile → Metronidazol/Vancomicina
non_cascade_iatrogenic.name_en: Broad-Spectrum Antibiotic → C. difficile Diarrhea → Metronidazole / Vancomycin
non_cascade_iatrogenic.ade_es: Infección por Clostridioides difficile, colitis pseudomembranosa, diarrea acuosa, inflamación colónica
non_cascade_iatrogenic.ade_en: Clostridioides difficile infection, pseudomembranous colitis, watery diarrhea, colonic inflammation
differential_hints: Antibiotic-associated diarrhea (non-C. diff, osmotic/secretory mechanism)
IBD exacerbation (Crohn's colitis or UC flare)
Viral gastroenteritis (norovirus, rotavirus)
Ischemic colitis (consider in patients with vascular risk factors)
Laxative overuse or osmotic diarrhea
Enteric infection from other pathogen (Salmonella, Campylobacter)
non_cascade_iatrogenic.note: Not a classic prescribing cascade: the antibiotic is treating an infection (primary indication) and the C. difficile treatment addresses a complication, not a misattributed symptom. Classified here for completeness in iatrogenic drug chains.
non_cascade_iatrogenic.recommended_first_action_es: Confirmar C. difficile con toxina en heces o PCR antes de tratar. En PVVIH, la infección por C. difficile es más frecuente y puede ser más grave. La vancomicina oral es ahora de primera línea; la fidaxomicina es preferida en casos recurrentes. Suspender el antibiótico causante si es clínicamente factible.
non_cascade_iatrogenic.recommended_first_action_en: C. difficile must be confirmed by stool toxin or PCR before treating. In PLHIV, C. difficile is more common and may be more severe. Oral vancomycin is now first-line over metronidazole; fidaxomicin preferred for recurrent cases. Stop the precipitating antibiotic if clinically feasible.
